predictions.text: Iran will issue an official, state-media-confirmed announcement recognizing Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader (or 'Leader of the Islamic Revolution'), following a formal decision by a competent body (e.g., Assembly of Experts).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if IRNA/PressTV/Khamenei.ir (or an official Iranian government statement) explicitly announces/recognizes Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader/Leader, with attribution to a formal decision/selection process (e.g., Assembly of Experts). Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: IRNA/PressTV/Khamenei.ir leadership announcements
Assembly of Experts meeting reports or communiques
Statements from senior Iranian officials (President/Speaker/IRGC) recognizing the new leader
predictions.rationale: If succession has effectively occurred, the regime typically seeks formalization to reduce uncertainty and consolidate authority, especially amid conflict.

predictions.text: ICE Brent front-month futures will settle at or above $150.00 per barrel on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if ICE Brent front-month settlement price is >= 150.00 USD/bbl on any trading day by the deadline (exchange settlement). Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Confirmed damage/attacks on Gulf export infrastructure
Sustained disruption to Hormuz traffic or insurance pricing spikes
IEA/OPEC emergency actions and public risk warnings
predictions.rationale: With oil already above $110, a further escalation or material shipping disruption could produce a tail-risk spike into the $150 area.

predictions.text: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) will close at or above 40.00 on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if VIX close (official Cboe close) is >= 40.00 on any trading day by the deadline. Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Equity index limit moves/circuit breakers in major markets
Large daily moves in Brent/WTI and credit spreads
Major escalation headlines involving Gulf infrastructure or U.S./Iran direct engagement
predictions.rationale: Oil-shock plus geopolitics can rapidly reprice risk; VIX>=40 is plausible if volatility propagates from Asia to U.S. equities.

predictions.text: U.S. Treasury’s TBAC materials will explicitly discuss stablecoins as a Treasury-market factor (holdings/flows/liquidity), with at least one dedicated slide or section titled with 'stablecoin(s)'.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if any TBAC presentation/report published on Treasury.gov by the deadline contains a dedicated slide or section header including the word 'stablecoin'/'stablecoins' and discusses their impact on Treasury bills/markets (not just passing mention). Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: TBAC quarterly presentations/agendas on Treasury.gov
Treasury/OFR remarks on nonbank buyers of T-bills
Stablecoin supply changes and disclosed reserve composition shifts
predictions.rationale: Growing stablecoin Treasury holdings are increasingly system-relevant, making TBAC discussion likely as Treasury monitors marginal buyers and liquidity dynamics.

predictions.text: South Korea will announce a new market-stabilization measure that includes equity-purchase capacity of at least 20 trillion won (₩20T) in total headline size.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official statement (Ministry of Economy and Finance, FSC/FSS, Bank of Korea, Korea Exchange, or government press release) announces a stabilization fund/program with stated capacity >= ₩20 trillion that includes stock/equity purchases (directly or via funds). Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: FSC/FSS emergency market measures announcements
Korea Exchange (KRX) volatility controls and short-selling policy changes
Government statements on stabilization funds or pension fund (NPS) actions
predictions.rationale: A sharp risk-off episode often triggers policy backstops; Korea has precedents for sizable stabilization vehicles after market stress.

predictions.text: China will announce additional national-level fiscal stimulus totaling at least 1.0 trillion yuan (CNY 1T) in incremental deficit, special treasury bonds, or local-government special bond quota increases beyond previously announced 2026 plans.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official announcement (State Council, Ministry of Finance, NPC Standing Committee decision, or Government Work Report addendum) specifies incremental 2026 fiscal measures >= CNY 1.0 trillion beyond the baseline 2026 plan, clearly described as additional/extra. Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: NPC Standing Committee budget/deficit decisions
MOF announcements on special treasury bonds and LGFV special bond quotas
Credit impulse and property-market stabilization policy escalation
predictions.rationale: Higher CPI and easing PPI deflation complicate monetary easing, increasing the odds of fiscal levers if growth or confidence deteriorates.

predictions.text: The U.S. federal government will experience a funding lapse (shutdown) lasting at least 24 consecutive hours.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if OMB, Treasury, or major wire services (e.g., AP/Reuters) report a federal funding lapse/shutdown lasting >=24 hours (partial or full) by the deadline. Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Continuing resolution negotiations and deadline countdowns
White House/Congress leadership statements conditioning funding on voter-ID legislation
CBO/OMB shutdown guidance or agency contingency plans
predictions.rationale: Heightened brinkmanship signals (tying funding to elections legislation) increase shutdown risk versus a typical appropriations cycle.

predictions.text: USDOT/FHWA will launch or award a multi-state pilot using satellite-based InSAR (or equivalent satellite deformation monitoring) for bridge or critical-structure monitoring covering at least 5 U.S. states.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if USDOT/FHWA publishes a press release, NOFO award list, or official program page by the deadline describing a satellite InSAR (or explicitly 'satellite deformation') monitoring pilot for bridges/structures spanning >=5 states (named or count stated). Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: FHWA NOFOs and award announcements for bridge/asset monitoring tech
State DOT procurement notices referencing InSAR or satellite deformation analytics
Vendor announcements of FHWA/statewide deployments
predictions.rationale: Demonstrated capability and strong safety ROI make near-term adoption in public infrastructure programs plausible.

predictions.text: A major academic publisher will publicly announce a single batch retraction of at least 200 papers citing 'paper mills', 'fake peer review', or 'fabricated' research/records as a primary reason.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a publisher (e.g., Wiley, Elsevier, Springer Nature, Taylor & Francis, SAGE, IEEE) issues a public statement/press release by the deadline announcing >=200 retractions in one batch/event and explicitly cites paper mills/fake peer review/fabrication. Otherwise NO.
monitoring_signals: Publisher research-integrity announcements and investigation updates
Retraction Watch reports of mass retraction events
COPE statements and large-scale journal audit disclosures
predictions.rationale: Ongoing integrity crackdowns and accumulated investigations make another high-count mass retraction event likely within a year.
